page: Microsoft Azure L32as_v4 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the Lasv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. This server features 32 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with multithreading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 256.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and a substantial local storage capacity of 7679 GB. The instance runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core performance, with the stress-ng single-core test and Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores placing in the top 25%. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and image processing also demonstrate strong capabilities. However, multi-core CPU performance under stress-ng and overall PassMark CPU scores fall within the average range. Large-block memory bandwidth and database operations also perform at average levels. For language model workloads, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds with small 135M models, though performance scales down to average for medium and large models.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the L32as_v4 offers a high density of local storage relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for data-intensive operations without requiring external storage attachments. The lack of a dedicated GPU limits its utility for heavy deep learning training, but its high memory capacity and local storage make it well-suited for large databases, big data analytics, text processing, and caching layers.
description: The Microsoft Azure L32as_v4 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Lasv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It features 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores, 256.0 GB of system memory, and a large local storage capacity of 7679 GB. Operating on Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance is Premium Storage capable but does not include a GPU. Benchmarks show strong single-core and multi-core Geekbench performance, alongside top-tier text generation speeds for small language models, while multi-core stress-ng and memory bandwidth remain average. This resource profile offers high storage density relative to compute, making it highly suitable for large-scale database operations, text processing, and data-intensive workloads that benefit from substantial local storage and memory.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L32as_v4 is a Lasv4-family virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 7679 GB of local storage, delivering strong single-core performance for database and data-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L32as_v4 is a Lasv4 virtual machine with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 7679 GB local storage, optimized for database workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity storage and memory-optimized virtual machine designed for demanding database operations and data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 32 vCPUs and 3.7 GHz clock speed
256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
7679 GB of high-capacity local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core and multi-core Geekbench benchmark results
Optimized for large databases, text processing, and data-intensive applications
